complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the PulseAudio fallback implementation into subtasks covering: 1) PipeWire availability detection mechanism, 2) PulseAudio initialization and connection handling, 3) Audio stream setup and configuration, 4) API compatibility layer with PipeWire implementation, and 5) Error handling and resource management
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves implementing a complete audio capture system using PulseAudio as a fallback when PipeWire is unavailable. It requires understanding both audio systems, creating detection logic, implementing a consistent API, and handling various edge cases. The code skeleton is provided but many implementation details need to be filled in. PulseAudio has a complex client/server architecture with modules that adds to the complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the Linux application-level audio capture implementation into subtasks covering: 1) Application enumeration with PipeWire, 2) Application enumeration with PulseAudio, 3) Application selection mechanism, 4) PipeWire application capture implementation, 5) PulseAudio application capture implementation, and 6) Graceful handling of application termination
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task is more complex than the PulseAudio fallback as it requires implementing application-specific audio capture across two different audio systems. It involves enumerating running applications with audio streams, selecting specific applications, setting up capture for those applications, and handling application termination. The task has dependencies on both PipeWire and PulseAudio implementations and requires maintaining compatibility between them.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the audio recording and file output implementation into subtasks covering: 1) WAV file format implementation with proper headers and data chunks, 2) Recording state management and buffer handling, 3) Recording limits implementation (duration and size constraints), and 4) File I/O optimization and error handling
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves implementing audio recording functionality with file output support. While the core functionality is straightforward, proper implementation requires handling different audio formats (initially WAV), managing recording state, implementing recording limits, and ensuring efficient file I/O. The code skeleton provides a good starting point, but details like WAV header construction, buffer management, and finalization need to be implemented.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the error handling and diagnostics implementation into subtasks covering: 1) Error type hierarchy implementation with platform-specific variants, 2) Diagnostic information collection and attachment mechanism, and 3) Error conversion utilities for platform-specific errors
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves designing and implementing a comprehensive error handling system. The complexity is moderate as the error type hierarchy is already defined using thiserror, but implementing proper diagnostic information collection across different platforms and ensuring consistent error handling throughout the library requires careful design. The task is foundational and will be used across the entire library.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the documentation and examples task into subtasks covering: 1) API documentation with rustdoc comments for all public items, 2) Platform-specific documentation for Windows, Linux, and macOS, 3) Example applications demonstrating key use cases, and 4) Troubleshooting guide with common issues and solutions
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Creating comprehensive documentation and examples requires understanding the entire library and its use cases. The task involves writing API documentation, platform-specific guides, example applications, and troubleshooting information. The complexity comes from ensuring accuracy across multiple platforms, creating working examples that demonstrate various features, and explaining complex audio concepts clearly. This task has dependencies on multiple other tasks being completed first.
